code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about a city (or related entities) into a single fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group triples by subject
    subject_triples = {}
    for triple in triples:
        if triple.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[triple.subject] = []
        subject_triples[triple.subject].append(triple)

    # Determine main subject (prefer city/county entities)
    main_subject = None
    for subj in subject_triples.keys():
        if any(keyword in subj for keyword in ["City", "County", "Texas", "California", "Ohio"]):
            main_subject = subj
            break
    if not main_subject:
        main_subject = triples[0].subject

    # Mapping from predicate to phrase template (subject omitted for relative clauses)
    phrase_templates = {
        "areaOfWater": lambda s, o: f"has a water area of {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"has a total area of {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"is in {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda s, o: f"sits at an elevation of {o} above sea level",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"is part of {o}",
        "populationDensity": lambda s, o: f"has a population density of {o}",
        "areaCode": lambda s, o: f"uses area code(s) {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"has a {o.lower()}",
        "areaOfLand": lambda s, o: f"has a land area of {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"'s leader is {o}",
        "utcOffset": lambda s, o: f"has a UTC offset of {o}",
        "populationTotal": lambda s, o: f"has a total population of {o}",
        "postalCode": lambda s, o: f"uses postal code(s) {o}",
        "largestCity": lambda s, o: f"'s largest city is {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"uses the {o}",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"is located at {o}",
        "chairperson": lambda s, o: f"'s chairperson is {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda s, o: f"'s headquarters is at {o}",
        "countySeat": lambda s, o: f"'s county seat is {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"'s capital is {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"people from {s} are called {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"includes {o} as an ethnic group",
        "state": lambda s, o: f"is in {o}",
        "populationMetro": lambda s, o: f"has a metropolitan population of {o}",
        "type": lambda s, o: f"is a {o}",
        "governmentType": lambda s, o: f"has a {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"is in the {o} time zone",
    }

    # Build clauses for each triple
    clauses = []
    for triple in triples:
        template = phrase_templates.get(triple.predicate)
        if template:
            clause = template(triple.subject, triple.object)
            clauses.append((triple.subject, clause))
        else:
            clauses.append((triple.subject, f"{triple.subject} {triple.predicate} {triple.object}"))

    if not clauses:
        return ""

    # Build complex sentence by connecting related information
    sentence_parts = []
    used_entities = set()

    # Start with main subject's clauses
    for subj, clause in clauses:
        if subj == main_subject:
            sentence_parts.append(clause)
            used_entities.add(subj)

    # Add related clauses with proper connectors
    for subj, clause in clauses:
        if subj != main_subject and subj not in used_entities:
            # Check if this entity is mentioned in previous clauses
            for part in sentence_parts:
                if subj in part:
                    # Convert to relative clause
                    if " is " in clause or " has " in clause:
                        parts = clause.split(" ", 1)
                        if len(parts) == 2:
                            sentence_parts.append(f", which {parts[1]}")
                            used_entities.add(subj)
                            break
                    else:
                        sentence_parts.append(f", and {clause}")
                        used_entities.add(subj)
                        break

    # Join clauses with proper punctuation
    if len(sentence_parts) == 1:
        sentence = sentence_parts[0] + "."
    else:
        # Connect all parts with commas and proper conjunctions
        sentence = sentence_parts[0]
        for i, part in enumerate(sentence_parts[1:], 1):
            if part.startswith(","):
                sentence += part
            else:
                sentence += f", {part}"
        sentence = sentence.replace(", ,", ",").strip()
        sentence += "."

    # Ensure proper capitalization
    if sentence and sentence[0].islower():
        sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

        "utcOffset": lambda s, o: f"has a UTC offset of {o}",
        "populationTotal": lambda s, o: f"has a total population of {o}",
        "postalCode": lambda s, o: f"uses postal code(s) {o}",
        "largestCity": lambda s, o: f"'s largest city is {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"uses the {o}",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"is located at {o}",
        "chairperson": lambda s, o: f"'s chairperson is {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda s, o: f"'s headquarters is at {o}",
        "countySeat": lambda s, o: f"'s county seat is {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"'s capital is {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"people from {s} are called {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"includes {o} as an ethnic group",
        "state": lambda s, o: f"is in {o}",
        "populationMetro": lambda s, o: f"has a metropolitan population of {o}",
        "type": lambda s, o: f"is a {o}",
        "governmentType": lambda s, o: f"has a {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"is in the {o} time zone",
    }

    # Build clauses for each triple
    clauses = []
    for triple in triples:
        template = phrase_templates.get(triple.predicate)
        if template:
            clause = template(triple.subject, triple.object)
            clauses.append((triple.subject, clause))
        else:
            clauses.append((triple.subject, f"{triple.subject} {triple.predicate} {triple.object}"))

    if not clauses:
        return ""

    # Build complex sentence by connecting related information
    sentence_parts = []
    used_entities = set()

    # Start with main subject's clauses
    for subj, clause in clauses:
        if subj == main_subject:
            sentence_parts.append(clause)
            used_entities.add(subj)

    # Add related clauses with proper connectors
    for subj, clause in clauses:
        if subj != main_subject and subj not in used_entities:
            # Check if this entity is mentioned in previous clauses
            for part in sentence_parts:
                if subj in part:
                    # Convert to relative clause
                    if " is " in clause or " has " in clause:
                        parts = clause.split(" ", 1)
                        if len(parts) == 2:
                            sentence_parts.append(f", which {parts[1]}")
                            used_entities.add(subj)
                            break
                    else:
                        sentence_parts.append(f", and {clause}")
                        used_entities.add(subj)
                        break

    # Join clauses with proper punctuation
    if len(sentence_parts) == 1:
        sentence = sentence_parts[0] + "."
    else:
        # Connect all parts with commas and proper conjunctions
        sentence = sentence_parts[0]
        for i, part in enumerate(sentence_parts[1:], 1):
            if part.startswith(","):
                sentence += part
            else:
                sentence += f", {part}"
        sentence = sentence.replace(", ,", ",").strip()
        sentence += "."

    # Ensure proper capitalization
    if sentence and sentence[0].islower():
        sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

    return sentence
